project.name: PropManager Finance Hub Optimization
project.description: Performance optimization for Laravel 12 + Vue 3 + Inertia.js property management Finance Hub

qualityStandards.description: This is production code for a property management SaaS. It must be maintainable, performant, and follow best practices. The codebase will outlive any single developer. Every shortcut taken becomes technical debt that compounds. Fight entropy. Leave the codebase better than you found it.
principles: Performance is a feature, not an afterthought
Optimize for Time to Interactive (TTI) and Largest Contentful Paint (LCP)
Database queries must be efficient - no N+1 queries
Vue components should be lazy-loaded where appropriate
API responses must be fast (<200ms for most endpoints)
Memory usage must be monitored for long-running processes

feedbackLoops.description: Run ALL feedback loops before committing. Do NOT commit if any fail.

commands.typecheck: npm run build -- --mode development 2>&1 | grep -i error

bundle_optimization.description: Vite bundle size and code splitting optimizations

items.title: Eliminate N+1 Queries in Finance Hub Controllers
items.description: Audit all Finance Hub controllers and ensure proper eager loading with ->with() for related models. Check Invoice, Payment, Tenant, and Building relationships.
steps: Install and configure Laravel Debugbar for query analysis
Navigate to each Finance Hub route and identify N+1 queries
Add ->with() eager loading to all queries fetching related data
Use ->withCount() for aggregate counts instead of loading collections
Verify with Debugbar that query count is reduced by 80%+
items.verification: Query count per page load should be < 15 queries

items.title: Implement Parallel Data Fetching in Controllers
items.description: Finance Hub Overview loads multiple statistics (This Month, Pending Amount, Collection Rate, Monthly Trend). These should be fetched in parallel, not sequentially.
steps: Identify all data fetching in Finance Hub Overview controller
Refactor sequential queries to use Laravel's parallel database helper or async where possible
Use Promise.all() equivalent pattern for independent data fetching
Consider using Spatie's laravel-data for DTOs

pattern.correct: Use Laravel's DB::transaction or parallel queries, or compute in single optimized query with subqueries

items.title: Implement Vue 3 Lazy Loading for Finance Hub Tabs
items.description: Finance Hub has 8 tabs (Overview, Billing, Expenses, Collections, Reconciliation, Reports, Templates, Settings). Only the active tab component should be loaded initially.
steps: Identify all tab components in Finance Hub
Convert static imports to defineAsyncComponent() with Vue 3
Add loading skeletons for lazy-loaded components
Ensure Vite code-splits these components into separate chunks
Verify with Vite bundle analyzer that initial bundle is reduced

items.title: Avoid Barrel File Imports - Direct Icon and Component Imports
items.description: Check for barrel file imports from icon libraries (lucide-vue, heroicons) and component libraries. Import directly from source files.
steps: Audit all imports for barrel file patterns (import { X, Y } from 'library')
Replace with direct imports (import X from 'library/dist/esm/X')
For Heroicons Vue: import from '@heroicons/vue/24/outline/IconName'
For Lucide Vue: import from 'lucide-vue-next/dist/esm/icons/icon-name'
Configure Vite's optimizeDeps if needed
items.impact: 15-70% faster dev boot, 28% faster builds, 40% faster cold starts

items.title: Implement Vue 3 Computed Property Caching and Memoization
items.description: Review Finance Hub components for expensive computations that should use computed() with proper dependencies, or be memoized.
steps: Audit all Finance Hub components for inline calculations in templates
Move expensive calculations to computed() properties
For non-reactive expensive functions, implement module-level caching with Map
Use shallowRef() for large datasets that don't need deep reactivity
Consider useMemoize from VueUse for complex memoization

items.title: Add Database Indexes for Finance Hub Queries
items.description: Add composite indexes for frequently queried columns in Invoice, Payment, and related tables. Focus on status, date ranges, and foreign keys.
steps: Analyze slow queries with EXPLAIN on common Finance Hub queries
Create migration for adding indexes: invoices(status, due_date), invoices(tenant_id, status), payments(invoice_id, status)
Add index for monthly trend queries: invoices(created_at, status, amount)
Consider partial indexes for 'pending' and 'overdue' statuses
Run ANALYZE TABLE after adding indexes

items.title: Implement Laravel Cache for Finance Hub Statistics
items.description: Statistics like 'This Month', 'Collection Rate', 'Monthly Trend' don't need real-time data. Cache these with appropriate TTL.
steps: Identify statistics that can be cached (Collection Rate, Monthly Trend, Overview counts)
Implement Cache::remember() with tagged caching for easy invalidation
Set TTL based on data freshness requirements (5-15 minutes for stats)
Add cache invalidation when invoices/payments are created/updated
Use cache warming for commonly accessed statistics

items.title: Move PDF Generation to Background Jobs
items.description: DomPDF is synchronous and blocks the main thread. Move invoice PDF generation to Laravel queues.
steps: Create GenerateInvoicePdf job class
Dispatch job when invoice is created/updated instead of synchronous generation
Store generated PDF path in invoice record
Add progress indicator in UI while PDF is generating
Implement retry logic for failed PDF generation

items.title: Implement Virtual Scrolling for Invoice Lists
items.description: For tenants with many invoices, implement virtual scrolling to only render visible rows.
steps: Install vue-virtual-scroller or implement custom virtual scroll
Replace standard v-for with virtual scroll for invoice tables with 50+ items
Add CSS content-visibility: auto for list items
Implement pagination with cursor-based pagination for large datasets
Add contain-intrinsic-size for proper scroll estimation

items.title: Optimize Inertia.js Shared Data
items.description: Review HandleInertiaRequests middleware for shared data that's loaded on every request but not always needed.
steps: Audit HandleInertiaRequests middleware shared() method
Use Inertia::lazy() for data that's only needed on specific pages
Remove unused shared data from global scope
Consider using Inertia::defer() for non-critical data
Profile shared data payload size

items.title: Implement Early Returns and Conditional Rendering in Vue Components
items.description: Ensure Vue components return early when in loading states to avoid expensive computations.
steps: Audit Finance Hub components for loading states
Add v-if checks for loading states before rendering expensive content
Use <Suspense> with fallback for async components
Implement skeleton loaders instead of spinners
Ensure watchers have early returns for unchanged values

items.title: Implement API Response Caching with Stale-While-Revalidate
items.description: For Finance Hub API endpoints, implement response caching with SWR pattern.
steps: Add Cache-Control headers to Finance API responses
Implement Laravel response caching middleware for read endpoints
Use ETags for conditional requests
Frontend: Use VueUse's useFetch with SWR or implement custom caching
Set appropriate max-age and stale-while-revalidate values

items.title: Configure Vite for Optimal Chunk Splitting
items.description: Configure Vite's rollupOptions for optimal code splitting, separating vendor code from app code.
steps: Configure manualChunks in vite.config.js for vendor splitting
Separate Vue, Inertia, and utility libraries into their own chunks
Use rollup-plugin-visualizer to analyze bundle composition
Ensure Finance Hub components are in separate chunks
Configure preload hints for critical chunks

pattern.config: build: { rollupOptions: { output: { manualChunks: { vue: ['vue', '@inertiajs/vue3'], vendor: ['axios', 'lodash-es'] } } } }

items.title: Implement Database Query Chunking for Reports
items.description: Finance reports may process large datasets. Use chunking to prevent memory exhaustion.
steps: Identify report queries that may return large datasets
Replace ->get() with ->chunk() or ->cursor() for large result sets
Implement streaming responses for CSV/Excel exports
Use database-level aggregations instead of PHP-level where possible
Add memory monitoring for report generation

items.title: Deduplicate Event Listeners and Watchers
items.description: Ensure global event listeners (keyboard shortcuts, window resize, etc.) are deduplicated across component instances.
steps: Audit for multiple addEventListener calls on window/document
Create shared composables for global event handling
Use VueUse's useEventListener with proper cleanup
Implement module-level Map for callback deduplication
Ensure all event listeners are cleaned up on component unmount

items.title: Defer Await Until Needed Pattern in Controllers
items.description: Move await/async operations into branches where they're actually used to avoid blocking code paths that don't need them.
steps: Audit controller methods for early awaits that block unused code paths
Move authorization checks before expensive data fetching
Return early on validation failures before querying database
Check feature flags before loading feature-specific data

items.title: Implement Read Replicas for Heavy Read Operations
items.description: For reports and statistics that don't need real-time data, configure Laravel to use read replicas if available.
steps: Configure database.php for read/write splitting
Mark Finance Hub read queries to use read connection
Ensure write operations use write connection
Test replication lag handling

items.title: Implement Preloading Based on User Intent
items.description: Preload Finance Hub tab data when user hovers over tab, not after click.
steps: Add @mouseenter handlers to Finance Hub tabs
Prefetch tab data using Inertia.prefetch() or manual fetch
Cache prefetched data in component state
Implement loading priority (current tab > hovered tab > others)

items.title: Implement Laravel Model Caching
items.description: Cache frequently accessed models like Building, PropertySettings that rarely change.
steps: Install spatie/laravel-model-cache or implement custom solution
Cache Building model with 1-hour TTL
Cache user property settings with appropriate invalidation
Add cache invalidation events on model updates

items.title: Optimize Tailwind CSS Build
items.description: Ensure Tailwind CSS v4 is properly configured for minimal production CSS.
steps: Verify content paths in tailwind.config.js include all Vue files
Check for unused CSS classes
Enable JIT mode if not already enabled
Analyze final CSS bundle size
Remove any @apply rules that could be direct classes

progressTracking.description: Track progress in progress.txt file, committed to repo after each task

format.blockers: Any blockers or notes for next iteration

metrics: API response times < 200ms for common endpoints
